Waste Trading: Our key customers comprise recyclers, fibre manufacturers, granule producers, and organizations focused on renewable resources to meet their energy objectives. We serve as a bridge between suppliers of recyclable and renewable materials and industries striving to minimize their environmental footprint. By enabling efficient waste-to- resource exchanges, we support businesses in advancing a circular economy and fostering sustainable industrial practices.

Restore Division: In this division, we procure recycled fabric from recyclers and transform it into value-added products such as curtains, bags, upholstery, apparel, and more. The Restore Division of RACE has played a pivotal role in fulfilling orders for domestic customers.

Innovation in Waste Manageme nt Technologies

Innovation in waste management presents a strategic opportunity for RACE to enhance sustainability, achieve regulatory compliance, and drive economic value. By leveraging advanced technologies and waste-to-energy solutions, we reduce environmental impact, optimize resource use, and gain a competitive edge.
The company has identified a significant opportunity in streamlining and organizing its currently unstructured supply chain. By undertaking this transformation, the company aims to strengthen its position as both a leader and a catalyst for structural change within the industry. The Procurement process has been defined as below stated-
Supplier Network & Collection
     Procurement has established a network of suppliers across India who are responsible for continuously collecting PET bottles.
     Before on-boarding, each supplier undergoes a proper KYC verification and site visit by the Race team.
Segregation & Baling Process
     Collected PET bottles are segregated into Natural, White, Green, and Multi categories before baling process begins.
     The bottles are then compressed in baling machines to form bales (150–350 kgs each), with deliveries. ranging from a minimum of 5–20 tonns to an average of 10–15 tonns.
Purchase Order & Demand Cycle
•     Recyclers issue Purchase Orders (POs) with rates and demand in tons, which are subject to change every 2–3 days.
•     These rates are communicated to the Procurement Team, and interested suppliers coordinate based on their ability to fulfill the demand.
•     Post the order, a vendor code is generated for the respective procurement member with their respective suppliers
Delivery & Documentation
•     Suppliers are given specific timelines to deliver material to recyclers.
•     At dispatch, documentation is verified by the Accounts Team, including E- way Bill, Geo-tagged truck photo, Invoice, Weighing Slip, and LR.
Quality Check & Debit Notes
•     Recyclers conduct random sampling to check for moisture, dust, contamination, or material mixing.
•     In case of defects, a Debit Note (DN) is issued and shared with the supplier; deductions are made upon acceptance as per agreement.
Payment & Compliance
•     Before releasing payments, the Operations Team verifies pending GST liabilities for each supplier.
•     Payments are released only after compliance is confirmed. If goods are rejected during quality checks, the material is returned to the supplier.
Continuous Operations
This procurement and recycling cycle is ongoing for both Biofuel and PET plastic, ensuring continuous supply chain efficiency.
An organized supply chain will foster greater transparency, improve supplier accountability, and ensure long-term resilience. Through this approach, the company aspires to set industry benchmarks in responsible sourcing while aligning with global ESG standards and supporting its broader commitment to sustainable growth.

Pollution prevention and control is a pivotal opportunity for RACE to enhance environmental stewardship, comply with stringent regulations, and safeguard public health. By adopting advanced technologies and responsible practices, we minimize emissions, reduce waste, and fortify our market position. This commitment also fosters innovation and drives long-term sustainability, ensuring we meet stakeholder.

By prioritizing recycling, reuse, and product lifecycle extension, we lower costs, minimize environmental impact, and strengthen our commitment. This shall not only supports our sustainability goals but also shall align us with evolving consumer and regulatory expectations, positioning us as a leader in responsible business practices.
Holistic Employee well - being related facilities & Occupation al Health & Safety, public Education and Awareness

By investing in educational initiatives and awareness campaigns on waste management for employees and value chain partners, the organization reinforces its role as a responsible corporate citizen, creates positive social impact, and strengthens stakeholder relationships.
Over the years, Race has consistently supported underprivileged communities— providing monthly food distribution to  nearly 300 laborers, ragpickers and marginalized groups each month for many years, while also sponsoring the education of six children of the same marginalized groups.
The company also places continuous emphasis on enhancing health and safety standards across its offices, ensuring a secure, healthy, and productive work environment.
These initiatives demonstrate Race’s commitment to holistic employee well-being and sustainable organizational growth. By prioritizing employee experience and workplace safety, the company further strengthens its reputation as an employer of choice, fostering resilience and long-term organizational resilience.

By investing in continuous learning and skill advancement, we foster employee engagement, improve productivity, and ensure alignment with industry best practices. This commitment not only supports individual growth but also strengthens our organizational resilience and productivity.

ESG governance is integral to maintaining stakeholder trust and achieving sustainable growth. Inadequate governance in environmental, social, and governance (ESG) practices can expose the organization to reputational damage, regulatory penalties, and operational inefficiencies. Moreover, failure to effectively manage ESG risks may lead to decreased investor confidence and hinder long-term strategic objectives.

As our base year of 2023, following are targets we achieved with the reasons mentioned below.
1. For Target 1 & 2- We have recognized and prioritized our value chain partners based on their level of engagement with RACE and accordingly refined our ESG assessment targets to ensure more effective implementation. To strengthen this process, sustainability checks for our suppliers have been developed, and ESG assessments will be progressively extended to cover a larger segment of our value chain partners over the next 3–4 years. While independent third-party assessments will continue to play a key role, we also plan to initiate internal ESG assessments in the near future, thereby creating a robust framework for continuous improvement and long-term sustainability.
3. For Target 2 & 3- By investing in educational initiatives and awareness campaigns on waste management for employees and value chain partners, the organization reinforces its role as a responsible corporate citizen, creates positive social impact, and strengthens stakeholder relationships.
Over the years, Race has consistently supported underprivileged communities—providing monthly food distribution to nearly 300 labourers, ragpickers and marginalized groups each month for many years, while also sponsoring the education of six children of the same marginalized groups.
The company also places continuous emphasis on enhancing health and safety standards across its offices, ensuring a secure, healthy, and productive work environment.
4. All our employees are 100% covered under insurance, and we are committed to maintaining this achievement. Ongoing efforts are focused on strengthening accessibility and ensuring continued comprehensive coverage for our workforce.
5. Our female workforce representation has not been on a higher side since attrition percentage was higher, however, we remain committed to strengthening workforce diversity through inclusive hiring practices and by fostering equal opportunities for all employees.
6. The minimum requirement is to have at least one woman director on the Board. We are pleased to confirm that this requirement is duly met, ensuring compliance with statutory obligations. While our board diversity remains consistent with last year, we maintain a well-balanced composition that reflects varied expertise and experience.
Our Board comprises a Chartered Accountant who is actively engaged in overseeing day-to-day financial operations, including regular review and monitoring of financial transactions for accuracy and compliance. We also benefit from the perspective of a Non-Executive Director with extensive experience in sales, general management, and strategies within the software and technology domain. In addition, one of our Directors is a seasoned entrepreneur and a recognized Quality Control Expert, leading a state-of-the-art export house catering to global markets in fabrics and related segments.
This diverse mix of professionals, along with an appropriate balance of Executive, Non-Executive, and a majority of Independent Directors, ensures that our Board composition is fully aligned with regulatory requirements while strengthening governance and strategic oversight.
7. We have achieved this goal by forming our ESG committee. This marks a significant step in our commitment to environmental, social, and governance initiatives. conducts quarterly meetings in FY 2024–25 to comprehensively review the company’s environmental, social, and governance (ESG) performance.

Our company, RACE ECO CHAIN LTD., is committed to the pursuit of sustainable business practices, and our voluntary adoption of the BRSR report is a testament to this dedication. We have diligently identified the pertinent ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) topics that hold significance for our business and have established our improvement targets with FY23 as our reference year. Within our industry, where unorganized participants dominate, we aspire to act as a transformative force by orchestrating the existing value chain and ensuring its sustainability. Simultaneously, we hold steadfast to our pledge of contributing to the social well-being of the underprivileged families who form an integral part of our value chain.

The Board of RACE, chaired by Mr. Sunil Malik, Managing Director, currently holds responsibility for the implementation and oversight of the company’s Business Responsibility policies. To strengthen governance, the company has established an ESG Committee with well-defined responsibilities.
The Board, along with the ESG Committee, conducts quarterly meetings in FY 2024–25 to comprehensively review the company’s environmental, social, and governance (ESG) performance. These reviews provide clarity on sustainability initiatives, potential risks, and overall alignment with industry standards and stakeholder expectations. The committee also develops strategies to enhance ESG performance and embed sustainability across operations.

Outcome- The ESG Assessment and BRSR initiatives are designed to strengthen the company’s reputation, ensure sustainable growth, and enhance transparency, while driving operational excellence, regulatory compliance, and community engagement. These efforts position the company as a resilient, innovative, and sustainability-driven industry leader.

At RACE, individuals, groups or institutions contributing value to its business chain are recognized as core or key stakeholders.

Our approach begins with a thorough assessment, recognising the importance of individuals and groups critical and essential for our operations and those impacted by our activities. RACE’s key stakeholders include bankers, community organizations, customers, local municipal bodies, employees, regulators and government agencies, shareholders, and suppliers.

We communicate with our team members through numerous platforms and channels, including, meetings, the Internet, internal messages, social media, blog posts, and newsletters that report on RACE’s sustainability efforts and other key business activities. We conduct employee surveys to gauge our team members’ views of the company’s vision and strategy, the work environment, work relationships, and job satisfaction.

At RACE, our approach for consultation begins with proactive stakeholder engagement, involving consultation rounds where stakeholders provide valuable feedback on key issues. This feedback is compiled and reported to our ESG Coordinating Group, led by senior executives and including site heads across various locations. This group plays a crucial role in facilitating ESG governance and ensuring that stakeholder perspectives meets our strategic decisions.

The ESG Coordinating Group works closely with relevant functions to align Company's approach with critical ESG topics.

It develops and approves annual ESG work-plans, reviews policy proposals related to ESG matters, and ensures ongoing communication with the Board regarding ESG activities and performance.
At the Board level, RACE provides essential oversight on ESG matters, integrating ESG risks and opportunities into our long-term strategy. They monitor progress against set milestones, ensuring that our ESG initiatives are aligned with Company policies, purposes, and stakeholder interests.

RACE actively engages various stakeholders to shape its Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) strategies, ensuring transparency and sustainable practices across our operations. We consult regularly with communities, customers, employees, and regulators to gather valuable insights on environmental and social impacts.

These inputs drive improvements in waste management, renewable energy adoption, and corporate social responsibility initiatives. Our ESG Coordinating Group integrates these perspectives into strategic decisions, aligning with evolving expectations and regulatory requirements. By fostering open dialogue, RACE reinforces its commitment to ethical governance, environmental stewardship, and social responsibility.

RACE conducts regular consultations with local communities in areas of operation, particularly focusing on vulnerable and marginalized groups to understand their environmental, social, and economic concerns.
Through these engagements, issues such as waste management, access to clean energy, and community development needs were identified.

In response, RACE implemented community-focused waste management initiatives, promoted renewable energy solutions to reduce environmental impact, and enhanced CSR programs targeting education, skill development, and healthcare for underprivileged groups. These actions ensure that stakeholder feedback directly informs ESG strategies, reinforcing inclusivity and long-term positive impact.

To strengthen our sustainability practices, we have introduced Electric Vehicles (EVs) into our operations. We started this initiative with the deployment of a Tata Ace 1- ton EV at one of our centers, dedicated to the collection of plastic PET bottles. This marks our first step in transitioning to low-emission logistics solutions. The use of EVs reduces our carbon footprint, lowers dependency on fossil fuels, and contributes to a cleaner urban environment. By integrating EVs into plastic waste collection, we are aligning waste management with green mobility. The initiative has also improved operational efficiency and reduced collection costs compared to conventional vehicles. The PET bottles collected through this program are processed for recycling, supporting our circular economy efforts. While this is a pilot deployment, it provides valuable insights for scaling EV adoption across more centers. In the coming years, we plan to expand this model, gradually building a larger EV fleet to maximize environmental and social benefits. This initiative reflects our commitment to responsible business, climate- conscious operations, and long-term sustainable growth.
Currently, the organization is not measuring its waste.

By proactively identifying and addressing business continuity risks, RACE has managed to enhance its resilience to disruptions, minimize the impact on operations, and ensure the continuity of essential services for their customers and communities.

1. Diversifying services and revenue streams across different waste management segments and customer bases.
2. Investing in resilient infrastructure and backup systems to ensure operational continuity and efficiency.
3. Managing supply chain relationships and contingency plans to reduce dependency and disruption risks.
4. Communicating and engaging with stakeholders to build trust and confidence in the Company's risk management capabilities.
5. Reviewing and updating insurance coverage and business continuity plan regularly to mitigate financial losses and improve preparedness.
